description: You are a mathematics tutor preparing a GeoGebra demonstration for a college student learning about complex numbers and roots of unity. Create an interactive visualization showing the five 5th roots of unity (solutions to z^5 = 1). The construction should feature a unit circle centered at the origin, five points representing the roots at angles 0°, 72°, 144°, 216°, and 288°, and a polygon connecting these points to form a regular pentagon. Include an angle slider (0° to 360°) and at least one text annotation such as '5th Roots of Unity' or 'z^5 = 1'. Save the completed construction to ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/roots_of_unity.ggb.
